briefdescription.para: default precision while comparing with Equal(..,..) functions. Initialized at 0.0000001.
maximal length of a file name

detaileddescription.para: /note linkage Something fishy about the difference between C++ and C in C++ const values default to INTERNAL linkage, in C they default to EXTERNAL linkage. Here the constants should have EXTERNAL linkage because they, for at least some of them, can be changed by the user. If you want to explicitly declare internal linkage, use "static".

briefdescription.para: the number of derivatives used in the RN-... objects.

detaileddescription.para: checks validity of basic io of is
Function to add two joint arrays, all the arguments must have the same size: A + B = C. This function is aliasing-safe, A or B can be the same array as C.

briefdescription.para: adds vector da to vector a. see also the corresponding diff() routine.

parameterdescription.para: vector a expressed to some frame w.
vector da expressed to some frame w.

simplesect.para: the vector resulting from the displacement of vector a by vector da, expressed in the frame w.

detaileddescription.para: returns the rotation matrix resulting from the rotation of frame a by the axis and angle specified with da_w.
see also the corresponding diff() routine.

parameterdescription.para: Rotation matrix of frame a expressed to some frame w.
axis and angle of the rotation expressed to some frame w.

simplesect.para: the rotation matrix resulting from the rotation of frame a by the axis and angle specified with da. The resulting rotation matrix is expressed with respect to frame w.

detaileddescription.para: returns the frame resulting from the rotation of frame a by the axis and angle specified in da_w and the translation of the origin (also specified in da_w).

see also the corresponding diff() routine.
R_w_a
Rotation matrix of frame a expressed to some frame w.
da_w
axis and angle of the rotation (da_w.rot), together with a displacement vector for the origin (da_w.vel), expressed to some frame w.
the frame resulting from the rotation of frame a by the axis and angle specified with da.rot, and the translation of the origin da_w.vel . The resulting frame is expressed with respect to frame w.

briefdescription.para: adds the twist da to the twist a. see also the corresponding diff() routine.

parameterdescription.para: a twist difference wrt some frame

simplesect.para: The twist (a+da) wrt the corresponding frame.

briefdescription.para: adds the wrench da to the wrench w. see also the corresponding diff() routine. see also the corresponding diff() routine.

parameterdescription.para: a wrench difference wrt some frame

simplesect.para: the wrench (a+da) wrt the corresponding frame.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Rall1d< T, V, S > &y, const Rall1d< T, V, S > &x)
memberdef.name: atan2

memberdef.argsstring: (const Rall2d< T, V, S > &y, const Rall2d< T, V, S > &x)
memberdef.name: atan2

detaileddescription.para: determines the difference of vector b with vector a.
see diff for Rotation matrices for further background information.

parameterdescription.para: start vector a expressed to some frame w
end vector b expressed to some frame w .
[optional][obsolete] time interval over which the numerical differentiation takes place.

simplesect.para: the difference (b-a) expressed to the frame w.

detaileddescription.para: determines the (scaled) rotation axis necessary to rotate from b1 to b2.
This rotation axis is expressed w.r.t. frame a. The rotation axis is scaled by the necessary rotation angle. The rotation angle is always in the (inclusive) interval $ [0 , \pi] $.
This definition is chosen in this way to facilitate numerical differentiation. With this definition diff(a,b) == -diff(b,a).

The diff() function is overloaded for all classes in frames.hpp and framesvel.hpp, such that numerical differentiation, equality checks with tolerances, etc. can be performed without caring exactly on which type the operation is performed.

parameterdescription.para: The rotation matrix $ _a^{b1} R $ of b1 with respect to frame a.
The Rotation matrix $ _a^{b2} R $ of b2 with respect to frame a.
[optional][obsolete] time interval over which the numerical differentiation takes place. By default this is set to 1.0.

simplesect.para: rotation axis to rotate from b1 to b2, scaled by the rotation angle, expressed in frame a.
- The result is not a rotational vector, i.e. it is not a mathematical vector. (no communitative addition).
- When used in the context of numerical differentiation, with the frames b1 and b2 very close to each other, the semantics correspond to the twist, scaled by the time.
- For angles equal to $ \pi $, The negative of the return value is equally valid.

detaileddescription.para:
determines the rotation axis necessary to rotate the frame b1 to the same orientation as frame b2 and the vector necessary to translate the origin of b1 to the origin of b2, and stores the result in a Twist datastructure.
F_a_b1
frame b1 expressed with respect to some frame a.
F_a_b2
frame b2 expressed with respect to some frame a.
The result is not a Twist! see diff() for Rotation and Vector arguments for further detail on the semantics.
determines the difference between two twists i.e. the difference between the underlying velocity vectors and rotational velocity vectors.
determines the difference between two wrenches i.e. the difference between the underlying torque vectors and force vectors.
Function to divide all the array values with a scalar factor: A/b=C. This function is aliasing-safe, A can be the same array as C.

Eats characters of the stream until the character delim is encountered
is
a stream
delim
eat until this character is encountered

Eats characters of the stream as long as they satisfy the description in descript
is
a stream
descript
description string. A sequence of spaces, tabs, new-lines and comments is regarded as 1 space in the description string.

Eats characters of the stream until the character delim is encountered similar to Eat(is,delim) but spaces at the end are not read.
is
a stream
delim
eat until this character is encountered

Eats a word of the stream delimited by the letters in delim or space(tabs...)
is
a stream
delim
a string containing the delimmiting characters
storage
for returning the word
maxsize
a word can be maximally maxsize-1 long.
Function to check if two arrays are the same with a precision of eps

simplesect.para: true if each element of src1 is within eps of the same element in src2, or if both src1 and src2 have no data (ie 0==rows())

detaileddescription.para: do not use operator == because the definition of Equal(.,.) is slightly different. It compares whether the 2 arguments are equal in an eps-interval
do not use operator == because the definition of Equal(.,.) is slightly different. It compares whether the 2 arguments are equal in an eps-interval
do not use operator == because the definition of Equal(.,.) is slightly different. It compares whether the 2 arguments are equal in an eps-interval
do not use operator == because the definition of Equal(.,.) is slightly different. It compares whether the 2 arguments are equal in an eps-interval
do not use operator == because the definition of Equal(.,.) is slightly different. It compares whether the 2 arguments are equal in an eps-interval
do not use operator == because the definition of Equal(.,.) is slightly different. It compares whether the 2 arguments are equal in an eps-interval
do not use operator == because the definition of Equal(.,.) is slightly different. It compares whether the 2 arguments are equal in an eps-interval

memberdef.name: hypot

briefdescription.para: outputs the IO-stack to a stream to provide a better errormessage.
pops a description of the IO-stack

detaileddescription.para: outputs one element of the IO-stack to the buffer (maximally size chars) returns empty string if no elements on the stack.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Eigen::MatrixXd &A, const Eigen::VectorXd &v, Eigen::MatrixXd &L, Eigen::VectorXd &D, Eigen::VectorXd &vtmp, Eigen::VectorXd &q)
memberdef.name: ldl_solver_eigen

briefdescription.para: Solves the system of equations Aq = v for q via LDL decomposition, where A is a square positive definite matrix.

detaileddescription.para:
The algorithm factor A into the product of three matrices LDL^T, where L is a lower triangular matrix and D is a diagonal matrix. This allows q to be computed without explicity inverting A. Note that the LDL decomposition is a variant of the classical Cholesky Decomposition that does not require the computation of square roots. Input parameters:
A
matrix<double>(nxn)
v
vector<double> n
vtmp
vector<double> n [temp variable] Output parameters:
L
matrix<double>(nxn)
D
vector<double> n
q
vector<double> n
0 if successful, E_SIZE_MISMATCH if dimensions do not match References: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cholesky_decomposition

memberdef.argsstring: (double alfa, double a, double beta, double b)

detaileddescription.para: Function to multiply all the array values with a scalar factor: A*b=C. This function is aliasing-safe, A can be the same array as C.

Function to multiply a KDL::Jacobian with a KDL::JntArray to get a KDL::Twist, it should not be used to calculate the forward velocity kinematics, the solver classes are built for this purpose. J*q = t

detaileddescription.para: Scalar product: I_new = double * I_old
Scalar product: I_new = double * I_old
calculate spatial momentum: h = I*v make sure that the twist v and the inertia are expressed in the same reference frame/point
Coordinate system transform Ia = T_a_b*Ib with T_a_b the frame from a to b.
calculate spatial momentum: h = I*v make sure that the twist v and the inertia are expressed in the same reference frame/point
Reference frame orientation change Ia = R_a_b*Ib with R_a_b the rotation of b expressed in a
Coordinate system transform Ia = T_a_b*Ib with T_a_b the frame from a to b.
Reference frame orientation change Ia = R_a_b*Ib with R_a_b the rotation of b expressed in a

detaileddescription.para: addition I: I_new = I_old1 + I_old2, make sure that I_old1 and I_old2 are expressed in the same reference frame/point, otherwise the result is worth nothing
addition I: I_new = I_old1 + I_old2, make sure that I_old1 and I_old2 are expressed in the same reference frame/point, otherwise the result is worth nothing

memberdef.name: pow

memberdef.name: pow

briefdescription.para: addDelta operator for displacement rotational velocity.

detaileddescription.para: The Vector arguments here represent a displacement rotational velocity. i.e. a rotation around a fixed axis for a certain angle. For this representation you cannot use diff() but have to use diff_displ().

a
: displacement rotational velocity
da
: rotational velocity
displacement rotational velocity
do not confuse displacement rotational velocities and velocities
do not confuse displacement twist and twist. IMETHOD Vector addDelta_displ(const Vector& a,const Vector&da,double dt) { return getRot(addDelta(Rot(a),da,dt)); } addDelta operator for displacement twist.

The Vector arguments here represent a displacement rotational velocity. i.e. a rotation around a fixed axis for a certain angle. For this representation you cannot use diff() but have to use diff_displ().

a
: displacement twist
da
: twist
displacement twist
do not confuse displacement rotational velocities and velocities
do not confuse displacement twist and twist. IMETHOD Twist addDelta_displ(const Twist& a,const Twist&da,double dt) { return Twist(addDelta(a.vel,da.vel,dt),addDelta_displ(a.rot,da.rot,dt)); }
axis_a_b is a rotation vector, its norm is a rotation angle axis_a_b rotates the a frame towards the b frame. This routine returns the rotation matrix R_a_b

briefdescription.para: to uniformly set double, RNDouble,Vector,... objects to the identity element in template-classes

detaileddescription.para: Function to set all the values of the array to 0

briefdescription.para: to uniformly set double, RNDouble,Vector,... objects to zero in template-classes

detaileddescription.para: To have a uniform operator to put an element to zero, for scalar values and for objects.
Sets the Wrench to Zero, to have a uniform function that sets an object or double to zero.
Function to subtract two joint arrays, all the arguments must have the same size: A - B = C. This function is aliasing-safe, A or B can be the same array as C.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MatrixXd &A, MatrixXd &U, VectorXd &S, MatrixXd &V, VectorXd &tmp, int maxiter, double epsilon)
memberdef.name: svd_eigen_HH

detaileddescription.para: svd calculation of eigen matrices

memberdef.argsstring: (const Eigen::MatrixXd &A, Eigen::MatrixXd &U, Eigen::VectorXd &S, Eigen::MatrixXd &V, Eigen::MatrixXd &B, Eigen::VectorXd &tempi, double threshold, bool toggle)
memberdef.name: svd_eigen_Macie

detaileddescription.para: svd_eigen_Macie provides Maciejewski implementation for SVD.
computes the singular value decomposition of a matrix A, such that A=U*Sm*V
(Maciejewski and Klein,1989) and (Braun, Ulrey, Maciejewski and Siegel,2002)

parameterdescription.para: [INPUT] is an $m \times n$-matrix, where $ m \geq n $.
[OUTPUT] is an $n$-vector, representing the diagonal elements of the diagonal matrix Sm.
[TEMPORARY] is an $m \times n$ matrix used for temporary storage.
[TEMPORARY] is an $m$ vector used for temporary storage.
[INPUT] toggle this boolean variable on each call of this routine.

memberdef.argsstring: (const SegmentMap::const_iterator it, const std::string &separator, const std::string &preamble, unsigned int level)
memberdef.name: tree2str
